title: El fin de semana
desc: What you did over the weekend

sn.s: Es lunes. Roberto me pregunta por mi fin de semana.
sn.e: It's Monday. Roberto asks about my weekend.
"What did you do over the weekend?"
I want to tell him, but in the past tense.

sn.s: "El sábado fui a la playa," digo.
sn.e: "On Saturday I went to the beach," I say.

sn.s: "Estuvo muy divertido. Nadé en el mar."
sn.e: "It was a lot of fun. I swam in the sea."
"I ate fresh fish with some friends."
"I watched a movie and slept a lot."

sn.s: Hablar del pasado es difícil, pero lo intento.
sn.e: Talking about the past is hard, but I try.

sn.s: "Hablás cada vez mejor," me dice Roberto.
sn.e: "You speak better and better," Roberto tells me.

sn.s: Fui, comí, vi, dormí. El pasado tiene su ritmo.
sn.e: I went, I ate, I saw, I slept. The past has its rhythm.

sn.s: Poco a poco, cuento mis propias historias.
sn.e: Little by little, I tell my own stories.
